0.q: McHB7 mainly functions in the pathway of photosynthesis, TCA cycle, and stimulus response. In the process of photosynthesis, McHB7 may regulate chlorophyll; pyruvate; glutathione; and proteins such as GSA1 and CHLP.
McHB7 protein was localized within the nucleus and OE of McHB7 in ice plant increased the abundance of Rubisco, indicating that McHB7 is likely a positive regulator of carbon fixation.

1.q: McSnRK1 interacts with McSKD1 via the C-terminal ubiquitin-associated domain (UBA) and kinase-associated domain (KA1) and catalyzes the phosphorylation of McSKD1 via the N-terminal catalytic domain.
McSnRK1 ... is a Ser/Thr protein kinase that contains an N-terminal STKc catalytic domain to phosphorylate McSKD1

2.q: McHB7 can improve photosynthesis, increase the leaf chlorophyll content, and affect the TCA cycle by regulating metabolites (e.g., pyruvate) and proteins (e.g., citrate synthase).

3.q: In OE plants, SOD and POD exhibited 1.4- and 2-times higher activities than WT under salt stress. McHB7 modulates the expression of stress-related proteins (e.g., superoxide dismutase, dehydroascorbate reductase) to scavenge reactive oxygen species.

4.q: The result indicates overexpressing McHB7 led to lower accumulation of reactive oxygen species (ROS) and stronger capability for ROS removal.

5.q: McCPN1 (copine 1), a RING-type ubiquitin ligase, has a central copine domain that interacts with McSKD1, and a C-terminal RING domain that catalyses protein ubiquitination.

6.q: Reciprocal coimmunoprecipitation studies revealed that the glycolytic enzymes interacted with the V-ATPase subunit B VHA-B

7.q: Reciprocal coimmunoprecipitation studies revealed that the glycolytic enzymes interacted with the V-ATPase subunit B VHA-B

8.q: Class I includes proteins that catalyse H+-coupled transport of K+ and Na+ with equal affinity (McNHX1 and McNHX3) and which mainly localize to the membrane of the vacuolar/pre-vacuolar compartments.

10.q: Roots expressing full-length McSnRK1 had better growth and lowered Na/K ratio compared to the wild-type or vector-only plants... The changes in Na/K ratio in transgenic cells and whole plants demonstrated the function of SnRK1 in controlling Na+ flux and maintaining Na/K homeostasis under salinity.

11.q: Expression of the protein in Escherichia coli and subsequent activity assays identified the protein as a novel myoinositol O-methyl transferase which catalyzes the first step in the biosynthesis of the cyclic sugar alcohol pinitol.

12.q: Expression of mcSKD1 was able to complement the K+ uptake-defect phenotype in mutant Saccharomyces cerevisiae, which is defective in high- and low-affinity K+ uptake.

13.q: aldolase was shown to stimulate V-ATPase activity in vitro by increasing the affinity for ATP

14.q: McCPN1 (copine 1), a RING-type ubiquitin ligase, has a central copine domain that interacts with McSKD1, and a C-terminal RING domain that catalyses protein ubiquitination.

15.q: McSnRK1 ... contains an N-terminal STKc catalytic domain to phosphorylate McSKD1, and C-terminal UBA and KA1 domains to interact with McSKD1

16.q: Knock-down SKD1 mutants were found to show abnormal root morphology and an imbalanced Na+/K+ ratio under salt stress, indicating that SKD1 is involved in the salt-tolerant mechanism in higher plants.

17.q: The changes in Na/K ratio in transgenic cells and whole plants demonstrated the function of SnRK1 in controlling Na+ flux and maintaining Na/K homeostasis under salinity.

18.q: subsequent activity assays identified the protein as a novel myoinositol O-methyl transferase which catalyzes the first step in the biosynthesis of the cyclic sugar alcohol pinitol.

19.q: McSOS1, belongs to the phylogenetic cluster of SOS1 transporters... AtSOS1 from A. thaliana is a well-characterized transporter of this family and catalyses Na+/H+ exchange at the plasma membrane

20.q: proteins involved in the regulation of transporters for vacuolar Na+ sequestration ... subunits of the vacuolar H+-ATPase V-ATPase

21.q: transgenic tobacco plants expressing McIMT1 gene coding for the inositol methyl transferase (IMT1) from Mesembryanthemum crystallinum has been shown to exhibit stress tolerance concomitant with stress-induced synthesis of pinitol

22.q: A cDNA encoding myo-inositol O-methyltransferase (IMT1) has been transferred into Nicotiana tabacum cultivar SR1. During drought and salt stress, transformants (I5A) accumulated the methylated inositol D-ononitol.

23.q: aldolase was shown to stimulate V-ATPase activity in vitro by increasing the affinity for ATP
